sourceMovie.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Sherlock Holmes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows clicked for you, Sherlock Holmes (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows?
faq.answer: Try Sherlock Holmes, Enola Holmes, Enola Holmes 2—each shares DNA with Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows?
faq.answer: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows (2011) is best described as Adventure, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Sherlock Holmes Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
